headline: 97% of tracked FPV cameras are Chinese-manufactured NDAA critical components, creating immediate compliance exposure for Blue UAS programs while motor and propeller supply chains show similar concentration risk above 70%.

lead_story.title: FPV Camera Supply Chain Shows Near-Total China Dependency on NDAA Critical Component
lead_story.body: 187 of 193 tracked FPV cameras in the Forge database are Chinese-manufactured, representing 97% concentration in a component class explicitly designated as NDAA critical due to imaging hardware with data storage and transmission capability. This exceeds even propeller dependency (96%, 463/484 units) and significantly surpasses motor concentration (70%, 212/303 units). Unlike propellers, which fall outside NDAA scope, FPV cameras directly trigger Section 889 restrictions, creating immediate compliance risk for any Blue UAS program using commercial camera modules without verified supply chain documentation.
lead_story.action: Audit all FPV camera modules in current and planned UAV bills of materials this week to identify China-sourced imaging components; flag any programs using commercial Runcam, Caddx, or Foxeer cameras for immediate NDAA compliance review.

sources.name: Forge parts database FPV camera analysis

supply_chain.signal: 97% China concentration (187/193 units) on NDAA critical component class; highest compliance risk in tracked database
supply_chain.window: Immediate — Section 889 restrictions already in effect
supply_chain.action: Source NDAA-compliant thermal or EO camera alternatives; budget 3-5x cost premium for US/allied imaging sensors; plan 6-9 month qualification timeline for new vendors

supply_chain.signal: 96% China concentration (463/484 units); HQProp, Gemfan, DAL dominate from Shenzhen/Guangdong manufacturing base
supply_chain.window: 12-18 months before tariff or export control pressure materializes
supply_chain.action: Identify 2-3 domestic or allied propeller manufacturers and run flight qualification tests on high-priority platforms by Q3 2026; stockpile 24-month supply of critical sizes for legacy systems

supply_chain.signal: 70% China concentration (212/303 units); T-Motor/Tiger Motors leads with 62 models from Nan'an City, Fujian
supply_chain.window: 6-12 months until alternative sourcing becomes constrained
supply_chain.action: Dual-source critical motor specifications with non-China vendors; accelerate qualification of KDE Direct, MAGiDRIVE, or European alternatives for new designs starting Q2 2026

supply_chain.signal: 467 airframes grounded or restricted across 25 states; $50M–$2B national exposure per Oregon/NASAO Revision 2 white paper
supply_chain.window: Active enforcement now — 23 states already restricting operations
supply_chain.action: Cross-reference agency drone inventory against NASAO restricted entity list; prepare replacement budget requests for non-compliant airframes in state-funded programs by end of Q2 2026

gray_zone.status: FCC Covered List entity with 0.850 critical gray zone score; app connects directly to DJI cloud infrastructure with identical encryption keys
gray_zone.development: Teardown analysis confirms SkyRover app maintains live connection to DJI cloud infrastructure with matching cryptographic keys, indicating data routing through adversary-controlled servers despite marketing as compliant alternative. OTA update pipeline touches adversary infrastructure, creating persistent supply chain and data security risk. Shell company filings link to SZ Knowact Robot Technology Co Ltd in Shenzhen.
gray_zone.buyer_exposure: Agencies operating S1 ($359) or X1 ($599) models face data exfiltration risk and potential FCC enforcement; estimated 200-400 units in state/local public safety inventory based on retail distribution
gray_zone.action: Ground SkyRover units pending network traffic analysis; migrate mission-critical operations to verified Blue UAS platforms; document all flights for potential data breach disclosure requirements

gray_zone.status: Texas DTPA lawsuit filed Feb 18, 2026 with 7 counts; no response filed after 45+ days, case frozen pending injunctive relief
gray_zone.development: Teardown confirms PCB layouts align exactly with DJI Mavic 3 Enterprise including matching board part numbers and relabeled RRC01 controller identical to DJI RC Pro. Firmware signed with DJI cryptographic keys giving DJI root key control. Texas AG seeking up to $10K per violation and injunctive relief; Anzu's non-response suggests potential settlement negotiations or operational wind-down.
gray_zone.buyer_exposure: Federal and state agencies with Raptor/Raptor T acquisitions face procurement fraud risk if DTPA violations are sustained; estimated $2M-8M in deployed units based on 2024-2025 sales velocity
gray_zone.action: Suspend new Anzu purchases until Texas AG case resolves; prepare alternative vendor justification for pending solicitations; agencies with existing Raptor inventory should document cybersecurity posture and implement network isolation for deployed units

watch_list.why: 40,000+ units confirmed in Geran-2/3 Shahed drones by Ukrainian military intelligence; creates potential for US export control enforcement against distributors
watch_list.trigger: Commerce Department BIS issues specific guidance on SBC sales to transshipment hubs or adds RPi to EAR99 monitoring list

watch_list.why: Autel challenging FCC Covered List designation and January 2026 retroactive authorization revocations; outcome affects $15M-30M in deployed Dragonfish and EVO platforms in public safety
watch_list.trigger: FCC denies reconsideration or DOJ files brief supporting FCC authority for retroactive equipment bans

watch_list.why: Identical PCBs to DJI Air 3 with same P1 chipset despite no acknowledged licensing; FCC Covered List status limits new sales but existing inventory remains in channel
watch_list.trigger: FCC exercises retroactive revocation authority on existing SPECTA certifications or Texas AG adds Cogito to DTPA enforcement action

predictions.event: FLIR Boson 640 tightens — 3 Boson units tracked, AeroV Switchblade ramp drives demand
predictions.hedge: Place long-lead orders for Boson 640 modules now with 50% deposit to secure Q4 2026/Q1 2027 allocation; qualify FLIR Hadron 640R as second source and budget $8K-12K premium per unit for dual-path procurement

predictions.event: Jetson Orin NX goes allocation-only for defense (13 boards in Forge DB at risk)
predictions.hedge: Secure 24-month forecast commitment from NVIDIA distributors by end of Q2; design upcoming platforms with Orin Nano fallback option; stockpile Orin NX 16GB modules for programs in production now

predictions.event: Mesh radio supply bottleneck — 29 options tracked, only 29 NDAA-compliant
predictions.hedge: Pre-qualify Silvus StreamCaster, Persistent Systems MPU5, and Rajant BreadCrumb for all mesh networking requirements; negotiate blanket purchase agreements with 90-day delivery windows before allocation begins

signal_summary.top_concern: NDAA critical component concentration in Chinese-manufactured FPV cameras
